data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Xiamen Yan Palace Bird’s Nest Industry Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1497) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Xiamen Yan Palace Bird’s Nest Industry Co., Ltd. declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 2.66 per 10 shares for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 11 May 2026 and payment set for 5 June 2026. The company also outlined key Hong Kong share register dates, including an ex-dividend date of 14 May 2026 and a book close period from 18 to 21 May 2026.

The updated announcement focuses on withholding tax arrangements for different categories of non-resident enterprise and individual H shareholders, generally applying a 10% tax rate subject to specific double tax agreements with the PRC. By clarifying tax treatment and reclaim procedures, the company aims to provide greater certainty to overseas investors regarding their net dividend receipts and to support smoother execution of its 2025 final dividend distribution.

More about Xiamen Yan Palace Bird’s Nest Industry Co., Ltd. Class H

Xiamen Yan Palace Bird’s Nest Industry Co., Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the bird’s nest products industry, focusing on the processing and sale of edible bird’s nest and related wellness food products. Its primary market comprises consumers seeking premium health and nutrition products in Mainland China and other markets accessible via its H-share listing.

Great Wall Motor Co ( (HK:2333) ) has issued an announcement.

Great Wall Motor has issued a supplemental announcement to its 2025 annual report to clarify the composition of its entrusted wealth management portfolio. The company now discloses that its entrusted funds primarily consist of low-risk R1-rated money market funds totaling about RMB 34.84 billion and low-risk bank wealth management products of about RMB 31.74 million, alongside RMB 90 million in principal‑guaranteed products with variable returns.

The revision replaces a previously generic disclosure and provides more granular information on the types and risk characteristics of the financial products used for cash asset management. Great Wall Motor emphasized that this additional information does not affect any other data in the 2025 annual report, and all other contents remain unchanged, offering stakeholders better transparency on how surplus cash is managed without altering reported financial results.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Ridge Outdoor International Limited ( (HK:2720) ) is now available.

Ridge Outdoor International Limited has announced new electronic dissemination arrangements for its corporate communications in line with updated Hong Kong listing rules and its articles of association. From 17 April 2026, most communications to shareholders will be provided electronically, with printed copies supplied only upon request.

Actionable corporate communications will be sent individually by email, and if a valid email is not available, a printed version and a request form for an email address will be mailed. Other corporate communications will be posted on the company’s and the stock exchange’s websites, and shareholders are encouraged to monitor these directly or request free printed copies if they have difficulty accessing online materials.

The company is asking shareholders to register functional email addresses via an online form or by contacting the Hong Kong branch share registrar, helping Ridge Outdoor streamline compliance and reduce reliance on paper. The shift underscores a broader market move toward digital investor communication, with implications for how shareholders access information and exercise their rights going forward.

More about Ridge Outdoor International Limited

Ridge Outdoor International Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2720. The company operates in the outdoor products sector, serving investors and shareholders through public markets in Hong Kong and complying with the Stock Exchange’s listing and disclosure requirements.

Twintek Investment Holdings Ltd. has announced significant changes to its board, with the resignation of independent non-executive directors Gu Feng and Wu Yi, who stepped down due to other business commitments but reported no disagreements with the board. data.title: Rongta Technology Audit Probes Deepen, Results Delay and Trading Halt Persist
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9881) ) has issued an update.

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. has disclosed further details on audit issues that have delayed publication of its 2025 annual results and led to a continued trading suspension in Hong Kong. The company said auditors are scrutinizing two sets of transactions involving a shareholder-guaranteed bank loan routed offshore and sizable prepayments for overseas advertising that were later refunded.

The first transaction centers on a RMB32.68 million bank loan to the group, guaranteed and effectively funded by its controlling shareholder and transferred via a related party into a Hong Kong fund before being fully settled. Auditors have questioned the commercial rationale, substance, approval process and regulatory compliance of this arrangement, despite the company’s assertion that it carried no risk to the group.

The second transaction involves about US$4.1 million in prepayments to an independent advertising agency to support overseas expansion, which were entirely refunded by year-end 2025. Auditors have raised similar concerns over the business rationale, internal approval procedures and adherence to internal controls and disclosure rules, leaving investors facing prolonged uncertainty over the company’s financial reporting and governance standards.

More about Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. is a mainland China-based company listed in Hong Kong, operating through a group structure and focusing on overseas business development. The company has outlined strategies to enhance its international presence and expand market share in key global markets, supported by planned marketing and financial arrangements for its overseas operations.

data.title: Mongolian Mining lifts coal output and gold sales in strong first quarter
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Mongolian Mining ( (HK:0975) ) is now available.

Mongolian Mining Corporation reported a robust first quarter of 2026 in its coking coal segment, with run-of-mine coal output rising 22% quarter on quarter and 32% year on year, while processed volumes also increased. Although washed coking coal production dipped 3% sequentially, sales volumes surged 60% from a year earlier, underscoring firm demand for its export-grade coal products and reinforcing its role as Mongolia’s leading washed coking coal producer.

In its gold and metals operations, ore processed at the Bayan Khundii mine rose 27% from the previous quarter, supporting a 20% increase in gold ounces sold and benefiting from a 17% higher realised gold price. The combination of higher volumes and pricing in gold, alongside strong coal sales, suggests improved operational momentum across the group’s portfolio, with potential positive implications for revenue diversification and resilience amid shifting commodity markets.

More about Mongolian Mining

Mongolian Mining Corporation is the largest internationally listed private mining company operating in Mongolia, with a diversified portfolio spanning coking (metallurgical) coal, gold, silver, copper and other non-ferrous metals. Its core coal assets include the Ukhaa Khudag and Baruun Naran coking coal mines in Umnugobi province, while its metals exposure comes via stakes in the Bayan Khundii gold mine and copper and silver deposits in Bayankhongor province.

Shareholders will also vote on the re-election of two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, the reappointment of KPMG as auditor, and a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, reinforcing governance continuity and providing flexibility for future capital-raising.

The proposed share issuance mandate, excluding treasury shares and specific existing schemes, would empower directors to allot new shares or grant related options within defined limits and time frames. This framework, which is standard among Hong Kong–listed companies, is designed to support CITIC Telecom’s funding agility for potential expansion or strategic initiatives while maintaining shareholder oversight and adhering to local regulatory requirements.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1883) stock is a Buy with a HK$3.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CITIC Telecom International Holdings stock, see the HK:1883 Stock Forecast page.

More about CITIC Telecom International Holdings

CITIC Telecom International Holdings is a Hong Kong–incorporated telecommunications group listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company provides telecom and related services, leveraging its position in regional and international connectivity to serve carriers, enterprises and consumers in Greater China and selected global markets.

data.title: China Power Subsidiary SPIC Hydropower Posts Lower 2025 Revenue but Higher Core Profit After Restructuring
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ).

China Power International Development has consolidated its control over SPIC Hydropower, now a 55.13%-owned subsidiary listed in Shanghai, reflecting the completion of an asset restructuring and strengthening its foothold in hydropower generation. This move broadens the group’s renewable energy base and aligns its portfolio more closely with China’s clean energy transition.

SPIC Hydropower’s 2025 audited results under PRC GAAP show revenue fell 15.9% to RMB 12.15 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders dropped 34.07% to RMB 532 million, though recurring net profit surged nearly 150%, and equity attributable to shareholders rose 3.73%. The company proposed a final cash dividend and cautioned that these figures reflect only SPIC Hydropower, not the entire group, signaling that investors should assess the data as a partial view of China Power’s overall financial position.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock, see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated power company engaged in the generation and supply of electricity, with a portfolio that increasingly includes hydropower assets. Following a recent asset restructuring, it holds a controlling 55.13% stake in SPIC Hydropower Co., Ltd., a Shanghai-listed hydropower operator, expanding its presence in China’s renewable energy sector.
